name: File Manipulation Mastery
description: Complete file reading, writing, and editing capabilities

tools.name: Read
tools.purpose: Read files with support for images, PDFs, Jupyter notebooks
tools.syntax: Read(filepath: str, limit?: int, offset?: int)
patterns: Can read images (PNG, JPG, GIF) and PDFs
anti_patterns: Never use cat/head/tail - use Read instead
Never read binary files as text

tools.name: Write
patterns: Must read first if file exists
Never create documentation unless explicitly requested
anti_patterns: Never write without reading existing files first
Never create new files when editing is sufficient

tools.name: Edit
tools.syntax: Edit(filepath: str, old_str: str, new_str: str, replace_all?: bool)
patterns: old_string must be unique in file
Use replace_all for renaming across file
Always prefer over Write for modifications

tools.name: MultiEdit
tools.purpose: Multiple edits in single atomic operation
tools.syntax: MultiEdit(filepath: str, edits: Array<{old_str, new_str, replace_all?}>)

tools.name: NotebookEdit
tools.syntax: NotebookEdit(filepath: str, cell_number: int, new_source: str)

workflow_patterns.name: safe_edit_workflow
steps: Read file first to understand content
Use Edit for single change, MultiEdit for multiple
Verify change by reading again if needed

best_practices: Prefer Edit over Write for existing files
Delete unused code completely - no compatibility hacks
